description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a prominent, rectangular solar panel array mounted on a structured base. The solar panel is composed of a grid of photovoltaic cells, with visible metallic framing and support structures beneath. The base appears to be made of a solid, possibly concrete or metal platform, with several raised sections and embedded components, likely for electrical or structural purposes. There are black and red accent elements, possibly indicating electrical connections or safety markings. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar panel system, suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or open public spaces. The structure is robust and engineered to support the weight and orientation of the solar panel for optimal sunlight exposure.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a metallic frame and solid base, featuring grid-patterned photovoltaic cells and structural supports. The installation includes raised sections and accent elements, indicating electrical or safety features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full grid of photovoltaic cells, metallic frame, and raised base sections with black and red accents.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panel and the thickness of the supporting base structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panel, support structures, and additional raised components on the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the panel's tilt and the arrangement of the base's raised sections.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth not clearly defined, appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metallic (likely aluminum or steel) frame and solid base
